title: DNA Extraction and mtDNA genome sequencing protocol from museum voucher specimens
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"dico4","text":"Museum voucher specimens represent an invaluable resource for biodiversity research, serving as permanent, verifiable records of species identities, morphology, and provenance (Suarez \u0026 Tsutsui, 2004). In recent decades, these archived specimens have also become critical molecular reservoirs, particularly for DNA-based taxonomic and phylogenetic studies (Wandeler et al., 2007). Among genetic markers, mitochondrial DNA (mtDNA) is especially suited for taxonomic assessment due to its maternal inheritance, relatively rapid mutation rate, and high copy number per cell, which enhances its retrievability even from degraded or historical samples (Avise, 2000; Paabo et al., 2004).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8633t","text":"In mammals, sequencing mitochondrial genomes or key regions (e.g., cytochrome b, COI) from voucher specimens enables retrospective species identification, clarifies cryptic diversity, and facilitates phylogeographic and conservation assessments (Tobe et al., 2010; Lorenzen et al., 2011). Integrating molecular data from authenticated voucher specimens strengthens taxonomic resolution and provides reproducibility across studies, underscoring their dual importance in both classical and modern systematics.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bkpc5","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cfcae","text":"Our protocol offers multiple extraction methods for variously stored samples (formallin or ethanol fixed). It makes it widely usable but the method was mainly tested on mammal samples. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a5f1k","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5h9fp","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fij33","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f1kpm","text":"References","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"covdk","text":"Avise, J. steps: Manually chop the tissue and soak in water at room temperature for 06:00:00 hours than remove the water.
Add 500 µL Alkaline lysis buffer (0.1 M NaOH, 1% SDS) to 1.5 mL screw-cap Eppendorf tubes with O-ring.
Wash tissue pieces 3 times with 400 µL nuclease free water for 00:30:00 minutes.
Cool down the samples on ice for 00:02:00 minutes.
Add 300 µL ATL buffer (pre-warmed to 98 °C ) to the tissue and incubate at 98 °C for 00:15:00 minutes in a heating block.
If the tissue is not digested completely, Proteinase K can be added up to 3 more times in 25 µL increments.
Add 40 µL Proteinase K and incubate for 48:00:00 hours at 65 °C .
For quality check the concentration was measured with Qubit fluorometer using the 1xds High Sensitivity kit.
For the library preparation we use the QIAseq FX DNA Library Kit (Qiagen) The handbook of FX DNA Library Kit. HB-2015-006_HB_QIAseq_FX_DNA_Library_0624_WW.pdf
Vortex the sample for 00:00:15 seconds and briefly spin it down.
Add 200 µL AL buffer (for both aliquot), vortex and centrifuge.
Split the sample into two tubes and proceed with both (load both on the same column).
Load both samples onto the same column, spin at 8000 rpm for 00:01:00 min.
Add 200 µL Ethanol (96–100%), vortex thoroughly and centrifuge.
Discard collection tube, place column in a new collection tube.
Add 500 µL AW1 buffer, spin at 8000 rpm for 00:01:00 min.
Place column in a new 1.5 mL tube and add 140 µL AE buffer to the column.
Place column in a new collection tube, spin empty at 14,000 rpm for 00:01:00 min.
Discard collection tube, place column in a new collection tube.
Add 500 µL AW2 buffer, spin at 14,000 rpm for 00:03:00 min.
Incubate for 00:30:00 min at room temperature, spin at 8000 rpm for 00:01:00 min. For more information you can found the kit handbook here: HB-2061-004_HB_DNY_Blood_Tissue_0623_WW.pdf
If you used this method for DNA extraction, go to Step 4 and proceed with quality check.
Add 500 µL Phenol/Chloroform/Isoamyl alcohol (25:24:1), mix gently for 00:05:00 min.
Cool down for 00:05:00 at room temperature.
Add tissue piece and incubate at 100 °C for 00:40:00 minutes.
Mix gently for 00:05:00 min and centrifuge again at 10,000 rpm for 00:05:00 min.
Transfer the upper aqueous phase to a fresh tube and add 500 µL Chloroform .
Centrifuge at 10,000 rpm for 00:05:00 min.
Centrifuge at 13,000 g for 00:30:00 min at room temperature. Remove the supernatant carefully.
Carefully transfer the upper aqueous phase and precipitate the DNA by adding 0.6–1x volume Isopropanol and 0.1x volume 3 Molarity (M) Sodium acetate .
Remove ethanol completely and carefully. Dry the pellet with open cap for 00:05:00 min.
Wash the pellet with 500-1000 µL 85% Ethanol , centrifuge at 13,000 g for 00:05:00 min.
Resuspend the pellet in 50 µL Nuclease-free water .
Buffer mix should appear yellow ( pH 7.5 ); if not, add 10 µL 3 Molarity (M) Sodium acetate.
Add 300 µL ERC buffer to each portion of 100 µL lysate.
Incubate tissue sample with 180 µL ATL lysis buffer (Qiagen) and 20 µl Proteinase K at 55 °C for 48:00:00 hours in a heat block.
Remove the water from the last washing step.
Transfer the sample to a MinElute spin column.
Centrifuge at 10,000 g for 00:01:00 min. Discard collection tube and reuse the column with a new sample portion (if applicable).
Wash bound DNA with 750 µL PE buffer. Centrifuge at 10,000 g for 00:01:00 min.
Discard flow-through and centrifuge the empty column at maximum speed. Place column into a new 1.5 mL tube.
Elute DNA with 50 µL EB buffer, incubate 1 min at room temperature and centrifuge at 10,000 g for 00:01:00 min. For more information you can found the kit handbook here: EN-MinElute-Handbook.pdf
For this version of extraction the DNeasy Blood and Tissue Kit (Qiagen) is used.
For this version of extraction the classical Phenol-Chloroform method is used.
If you used this method for DNA extraction, go to Step 4 and proceed with quality check.
For this version of extraction the DNeasy Blood and Tissue Kit (Qiagen) is used.
Aliquot 199 µL 1x Working solution to Qubit assay tube (equal to the number of your samples). Prepare two extra tubes for the standards with 190 µL 1x Working solution.
Add 1 µL sample for each test tube and 10 µL from the Standards (Standard 1, Standard 2). The final volume is 200 µL
Calibrate Qubit fluorometer with the standards based on manufacturers recommendation.
Mix each sample vigorously by vortexing and pulse centrifuge to collect the liquid. Incubate at room temperature for 00:02:00 min before measuring.
Read your sample. If it is possible, fragment length could be checked on Bioanalyzer or Tapestation system.

Fragmentation and End preparation Prepare the following reaction: Component Volume FX Buffer 10x 5 µL FX Enzyme Mix 10 µL Sample DNA ( 100 ng ) 35 µL Final volume: 50 µL Incubate the reaction with the following conditions: Temperature Time 4 °C 00:01:00 32 °C 00:01:00 65 °C 00:30:00 4 °C DNA fragmentation was minimised due to the high degradation of nucleic acid (DNA ≤ 500 bp) material and low concentration (≤ 3 ng /µl).
Sample dilution We need 100 ng DNA input for library preparation. Dilute the sample with Nuclease-free water to 100 ng / 35 µL concentration.

Library Quantification and checking Measure the concentration with Qubit Fluorometer according to Step 4 . If it is possible fragment length could be checked on Bioanalyzer or Tapestation system. Alternative solution is gel electrophoresis (1.5% gel), as the library concentration should be high enough.

Start sequencing Pool the sequencing based on the applied flow cell and equipment and start the run. Mitochondrial DNA typically constitutes <0.5–1% of total reads in whole-genome Illumina shotgun libraries. The minimum sequencing depth for successful recovery of complete mitochondrial genome is around 10 million reads/sample.
Clean up Clean up the adapter ligated product with Ampure Xp Beads

Adapter Ligation Prepare the following reaction: Component Volume DNA-Ligase Buffer 5x 20 µL DNA-Ligase 10 µL Nuclease free H2O 15 µL Unique Adapter 5 µL Previous reaction 50 µL Final volume: 100 µL Incubate at 20 °C for 00:15:00 minutes.

Library amplification Prepare the following reaction Component Volume HIFI PCR Master Mix 2x 25 µL adapter Primer Mix 1.5 µL Cleaned up DNA 23.5 µL Final volume 50 µL Reaction conditions: Temperature Time No. of cycles 98 °C 00:02:00 1 98 °C 00:00:20 8 60 °C 00:00:30 8 72 °C 00:00:30 8 72 °C 00:01:00 1 4 °C Till further processing
Remove 50 µL of the supernatant and transfer to a new PCR tube.
Add 52.5 µL elution buffer to the dried beads and resuspend well. Incubate for 00:02:00 minutes off the magnetic stand. Place back to the magnetic stand and incubate for additional 00:02:00 .
Allow the beads to dry on the magnetic stand for approx. 5 min (till the point when the pellet lose its shine).
Repeat the washing step: Wash the beads with 200 µL 80% EtOH for 00:00:30 seconds and and carefully remove the supernatant.
Wash the beads with 200 µL 80% EtOH for 00:00:30 seconds and and carefully remove the supernatant.
Place on magnetic stand for 00:02:00 and carefully remove the supernatant.
Mix 80 µL beads with the previous reaction and incubate for 00:05:00 minutes at room temperature.
Library Clean up Clean up the amplified library with Ampure Xp Beads
Mix 50 µL beads with the previous reaction and incubate for 00:05:00 minutes at room temperature.
Place on magnetic stand for 00:02:00 and carefully remove the supernatant.
Wash the beads with 200 µL 80% EtOH for 00:00:30 seconds and and carefully remove the supernatant.
Repeat the washing step: Wash the beads with 200 µL 80% EtOH for 00:00:30 seconds and and carefully remove the supernatant.
Allow the beads to dry on the magnetic stand for approx. 5 min (till the point when the pellet lose its shine).
Add 52.5 µL elution buffer to the dried beads and resuspend well. Incubate for 00:02:00 minutes off the magnetic stand. Place back to the magnetic stand and incubate for additional 00:02:00 .
Remove 50 µL of the supernatant and transfer to a new PCR tube. Storage at 4 °C for a day is possible but for long term storage -20 °C is suitable.
